{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Engineering – Materials Lead III",
  "description": "Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil &amp; gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities &acirc;&euro;&ldquo; with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Engineering &ndash; Materials Lead III

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly experienced Materials Lead to manage and coordinate the flow of materials, equipment, and components across project sites, ensuring alignment with delivery schedules, project priorities, and operational requirements.
	This role is responsible for overseeing materials management processes, ensuring accurate tracking, storage, and movement of materials across departments and sites.
	The Materials Lead also ensures that all records are properly maintained and supports project delivery through effective coordination and control.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee and maintain accurate records of material inventory, delivery, shipping, storage, and location status
	Monitor and coordinate the movement of materials across departments and project sites
	Ensure proper arrangement for repair and/or assembly of company-provided materials and equipment
	Supervise material handling processes to align with project timelines and priorities
	Support Project and Site Managers with materials-related requirements
	Ensure compliance with quality control procedures and standards


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s or Master&rsquo;s degree in a relevant field
	Proven experience in materials management, preferably within the Oil &amp; Gas industry
	Strong knowledge of industry equipment and materials identification
	Extensive experience with SAP material catalogs and search tools
	Good understanding of plant operations and material usage
	Knowledge of quality control systems and procedures
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, Outlook, Access)
	Strong time management, organizational, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Ability to influence stakeholders and work across teams


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Resume&nbsp;to:&nbsp;hesprojectrecruitment@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  },

Engineering – Materials Lead III Job at Habrams Energy




        
    

        
        Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil & gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities â€“ with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Engineering – Materials Lead III

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly experienced Materials Lead to manage and coordinate the flow of materials, equipment, and components across project sites, ensuring alignment with delivery schedules, project priorities, and operational requirements.
	This role is responsible for overseeing materials management processes, ensuring accurate tracking, storage, and movement of materials across departments and sites.
	The Materials Lead also ensures that all records are properly maintained and supports project delivery through effective coordination and control.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee and maintain accurate records of material inventory, delivery, shipping, storage, and location status
	Monitor and coordinate the movement of materials across departments and project sites
	Ensure proper arrangement for repair and/or assembly of company-provided materials and equipment
	Supervise material handling processes to align with project timelines and priorities
	Support Project and Site Managers with materials-related requirements
	Ensure compliance with quality control procedures and standards


Requirements


	Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field
	Proven experience in materials management, preferably within the Oil & Gas industry
	Strong knowledge of industry equipment and materials identification
	Extensive experience with SAP material catalogs and search tools
	Good understanding of plant operations and material usage
	Knowledge of quality control systems and procedures
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, Outlook, Access)
	Strong time management, organizational, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Ability to influence stakeholders and work across teams


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Manager",
  "description": "Apple &amp; Pears&nbsp;Limited was incorporated in the year 2012 as an Agro Allied &amp; Food Industry to produce soya meal, concentrate and finished feed for the poultry industry, and refined edible oil, mayonnaise, ketchup, salad cream, vinegar,custard powder and Chocolate Drink for the Nigerian market.

The company has invested millions of dollars in land, building, construction of infrastructural facilities and machinery, with one goal in mind and that is to have an integrated facility encompassing the complete value chain of the soya bean business, from sourcing, processing, extraction, refining, packaging and distribution of a wide range of premium products. In response to environmental concerns, we have installed solar panels to partially power the plant on&nbsp;solar energy&nbsp;also we have a state of the art effluent treatment plant to treat and reuse the water discharged during the various production processes.

We have created strong brands such as Laziz Oil, Winner Soya Oil, Active Oil, La Mayo Mayonnaise, Laziz Ketchup, Laziz Salad Cream, Laziz Thousand Island, Laziz Vinegar, Laziz Custard, Laziz Chocolate Drink, A1 Soya Bean Meal, A1 Soya Lecithin, HI Pro Concentrate and HI Pro Feed.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Sagamu, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Apple &amp; Pears Limited, a food processing and manufacturing company, is seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Procurement Manager to manage all purchasing activities and report to the Head - Supply Chain.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for sourcing quality materials, managing vendor relationships, and ensuring cost-effective procurement processes that support efficient production operations.


Job Description
The Procurement Manager will be responsible for:


	Sourcing and procuring raw materials, equipment, and other supplies required for production and operations.
	Identifying, evaluating, and vetting vendors to ensure reliability, quality, and compliance with company standards.
	Negotiating pricing, terms, and contracts with suppliers to achieve cost savings and value for money.
	Drawing up and managing agreements/contracts with vendors.
	Monitoring supplier performance and maintaining strong supplier relationships.
	Ensuring timely delivery of materials to avoid production delays.
	Maintaining accurate procurement records and documentation.
	Collaborating with internal departments to understand procurement needs and forecast demand.
	Ensuring compliance with company policies and relevant regulatory requirements.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Procurement, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	4&ndash;6 years of proven experience in procurement or supply chain management, preferably within a manufacturing or food processing environment.
	Strong negotiation, analytical, and vendor management skills.
	Good knowledge of procurement processes, contract management, and supplier evaluation.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and procurement/ERP systems.
	High level of integrity, attention to detail, and organizational skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Applicants residing within Ogun State and its environs will have an added advantage.
	Must be able to resume on short notice.


Salary
N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;hr1appleandpears@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as subject of email.",

Procurement Manager Job at Apple and Pears Limited




        
    

        
        Apple & Pears Limited was incorporated in the year 2012 as an Agro Allied & Food Industry to produce soya meal, concentrate and finished feed for the poultry industry, and refined edible oil, mayonnaise, ketchup, salad cream, vinegar,custard powder and Chocolate Drink for the Nigerian market.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



The company has invested millions of dollars in land, building, construction of infrastructural facilities and machinery, with one goal in mind and that is to have an integrated facility encompassing the complete value chain of the soya bean business, from sourcing, processing, extraction, refining, packaging and distribution of a wide range of premium products. In response to environmental concerns, we have installed solar panels to partially power the plant on solar energy also we have a state of the art effluent treatment plant to treat and reuse the water discharged during the various production processes.

We have created strong brands such as Laziz Oil, Winner Soya Oil, Active Oil, La Mayo Mayonnaise, Laziz Ketchup, Laziz Salad Cream, Laziz Thousand Island, Laziz Vinegar, Laziz Custard, Laziz Chocolate Drink, A1 Soya Bean Meal, A1 Soya Lecithin, HI Pro Concentrate and HI Pro Feed.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Manager

Job Location: Sagamu, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Apple & Pears Limited, a food processing and manufacturing company, is seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Procurement Manager to manage all purchasing activities and report to the Head - Supply Chain.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for sourcing quality materials, managing vendor relationships, and ensuring cost-effective procurement processes that support efficient production operations.


Job Description
The Procurement Manager will be responsible for:


	Sourcing and procuring raw materials, equipment, and other supplies required for production and operations.
	Identifying, evaluating, and vetting vendors to ensure reliability, quality, and compliance with company standards.
	Negotiating pricing, terms, and contracts with suppliers to achieve cost savings and value for money.
	Drawing up and managing agreements/contracts with vendors.
	Monitoring supplier performance and maintaining strong supplier relationships.
	Ensuring timely delivery of materials to avoid production delays.
	Maintaining accurate procurement records and documentation.
	Collaborating with internal departments to understand procurement needs and forecast demand.
	Ensuring compliance with company policies and relevant regulatory requirements.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Procurement, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	4–6 years of proven experience in procurement or supply chain management, preferably within a manufacturing or food processing environment.
	Strong negotiation, analytical, and vendor management skills.
	Good knowledge of procurement processes, contract management, and supplier evaluation.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and procurement/ERP systems.
	High level of integrity, attention to detail, and organizational skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Applicants residing within Ogun State and its environs will have an added advantage.
	Must be able to resume on short notice.


Salary
N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control and Kitchen Supervisor",
  "description": "At Firewood Rice Nigeria, we don&rsquo;t just serve meals - we serve memories. With a deep-rooted love for authentic Nigerian dishes, we&rsquo;ve built a brand that delivers comfort, flavor, and joy to our customers across Lagos. Our energetic team is the secret sauce behind our success, and we&rsquo;re growing fast! If you love food, innovation, and working in a vibrant, forward-thinking environment, we want you with us.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control and Kitchen Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Key Responsibilities


	Develop daily, weekly, and monthly production schedules based on outlet demand, bulk orders, and sales forecasts.
	Direct and oversee the daily activities of kitchen staff including cooks, chefs, and kitchen assistants.
	Assign tasks, monitor workflow, and ensure that kitchen operations run efficiently and in accordance with company standards.
	Supervise food preparation and cooking processes to ensure consistency in taste, presentation, and portion control.
	Coordinate kitchen schedules to ensure adequate staffing and smooth production flow.
	Monitor food preparation processes to ensure compliance with established quality standards and approved recipes.
	Inspect raw materials and ingredients to ensure freshness, quality, and proper storage.
	Conduct routine quality checks on food products before distribution to outlets, corporate clients, and takeout customers.
	Enforce strict adherence to food safety regulations, hygiene practices, and sanitation standards across the kitchen.
	Identify quality issues and implement corrective actions to maintain product standards.
	Train new kitchen staff on food preparation standards, hygiene practices, and operational procedures.
	Monitor kitchen inventory levels and coordinate with the inventory team to ensure adequate supply of ingredients.
	Oversee the proper use and maintenance of kitchen equipment and report faults when necessary.
	Monitor kitchen output to ensure timely preparation and dispatch of food orders.
	Ensure that food prepared for restaurants, corporate clients, and events meets company quality standards.
	Conduct regular kitchen inspections to identify and correct food safety or operational risks.
	Ensure full compliance with internal policies and relevant food safety regulations.


Qualifications


	HND/BSc in Food Science, Hospitality Management, Catering, or a related field with&nbsp;2&ndash;4 years of experience in kitchen operations, food production, or quality control within a food service environment.
	Knowledge of food safety regulations and quality assurance practices is an advantage.
	Strong leadership, planning, and reporting skills.
	Knowledge of food safety and hygiene standards.
	Ability to work under pressure and manage tight production timelines.


Compensation, Scope of Work, and Benefits


	Salary: N200,000 / Month
	Days of Work: Monday &ndash; Saturday
	Working Hours: 8:00 AM &ndash; 5:00 PM
	Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) coverage


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to:&nbsp;firewoodjollofcareers@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Quality Control and Kitchen Supervisor Job at Firewood Rice Nigeria




        
    

        
        At Firewood Rice Nigeria, we don’t just serve meals - we serve memories. With a deep-rooted love for authentic Nigerian dishes, we’ve built a brand that delivers comfort, flavor, and joy to our customers across Lagos. Our energetic team is the secret sauce behind our success, and we’re growing fast! If you love food, innovation, and working in a vibrant, forward-thinking environment, we want you with us.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control and Kitchen Supervisor

Job Location: Lagos

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop daily, weekly, and monthly production schedules based on outlet demand, bulk orders, and sales forecasts.
	Direct and oversee the daily activities of kitchen staff including cooks, chefs, and kitchen assistants.
	Assign tasks, monitor workflow, and ensure that kitchen operations run efficiently and in accordance with company standards.
	Supervise food preparation and cooking processes to ensure consistency in taste, presentation, and portion control.
	Coordinate kitchen schedules to ensure adequate staffing and smooth production flow.
	Monitor food preparation processes to ensure compliance with established quality standards and approved recipes.
	Inspect raw materials and ingredients to ensure freshness, quality, and proper storage.
	Conduct routine quality checks on food products before distribution to outlets, corporate clients, and takeout customers.
	Enforce strict adherence to food safety regulations, hygiene practices, and sanitation standards across the kitchen.
	Identify quality issues and implement corrective actions to maintain product standards.
	Train new kitchen staff on food preparation standards, hygiene practices, and operational procedures.
	Monitor kitchen inventory levels and coordinate with the inventory team to ensure adequate supply of ingredients.
	Oversee the proper use and maintenance of kitchen equipment and report faults when necessary.
	Monitor kitchen output to ensure timely preparation and dispatch of food orders.
	Ensure that food prepared for restaurants, corporate clients, and events meets company quality standards.
	Conduct regular kitchen inspections to identify and correct food safety or operational risks.
	Ensure full compliance with internal policies and relevant food safety regulations.


Qualifications


	HND/BSc in Food Science, Hospitality Management, Catering, or a related field with 2–4 years of experience in kitchen operations, food production, or quality control within a food service environment.
	Knowledge of food safety regulations and quality assurance practices is an advantage.
	Strong leadership, planning, and reporting skills.
	Knowledge of food safety and hygiene standards.
	Ability to work under pressure and manage tight production timelines.


Compensation, Scope of Work, and Benefits


	Salary: N200,000 / Month
	Days of Work: Monday – Saturday
	Working Hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
	Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) coverage


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accounts Payable Officer",
  "description": "LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company&rsquo;s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000+ medical products to 1000+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology-led healthcare system for the future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Payable Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Remote
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Finance
Reports To: Finance Manager

Job Summary


	The Accounts Payable Officer will be responsible for managing LifeBank&rsquo;s payables process, ensuring timely and accurate payments to vendors, partners, and service providers.
	This role is critical in supporting seamless operations across procurement, logistics, and healthcare delivery by maintaining strong financial controls and vendor relationships.


Key Responsibilities


	Review, verify, and process vendor invoices, ensuring proper documentation and approvals
	Log all approved payables accurately on Zero (accounting system) in a timely manner
	Prepare and execute payments (bank transfers, payment platforms) accurately and on schedule
	Manage high-volume, time-sensitive payments related to logistics, medical supplies, and operations
	Reconcile vendor statements and resolve discrepancies promptly
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all payable transactions
	Liaise with vendors, internal teams (Procurement, Operations), and Finance to ensure smooth payment processes
	Track outstanding payables and provide regular reports to the Finance Manager
	Ensure compliance with company policies, donor requirements (where applicable), and regulatory standards
	Support tax processes including withholding tax deductions and documentation
	Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities
	Support audits by providing required documentation and schedules


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	2&ndash;4 years experience in accounts payable or a similar role, preferably in a fast-paced environment
	Strong understanding of accounting principles and payables processes
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting/ERP systems (experience with Zero is an advantage)
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy
	Good communication and stakeholder management skills
	Ability to prioritize and manage multiple deadlines.


Preferred Qualifications:


	ICAN/ACCA (or in progress)
	Experience in healthcare, logistics, or a high-growth startup
	Familiarity with payment platforms and reconciliation tools.


Key Competencies:


	High level of integrity and accountability
	Strong organizational skills
	Problem-solving and critical thinking
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Collaboration and teamwork.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs / Resume to:&nbsp;team@lifebank.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-31",

Accounts Payable Officer Job at LifeBank




        
    

        
        LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company’s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000+ medical products to 1000+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology-led healthcare system for the future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Payable Officer

Job Location: Remote
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Finance
Reports To: Finance Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	The Accounts Payable Officer will be responsible for managing LifeBank’s payables process, ensuring timely and accurate payments to vendors, partners, and service providers.
	This role is critical in supporting seamless operations across procurement, logistics, and healthcare delivery by maintaining strong financial controls and vendor relationships.


Key Responsibilities


	Review, verify, and process vendor invoices, ensuring proper documentation and approvals
	Log all approved payables accurately on Zero (accounting system) in a timely manner
	Prepare and execute payments (bank transfers, payment platforms) accurately and on schedule
	Manage high-volume, time-sensitive payments related to logistics, medical supplies, and operations
	Reconcile vendor statements and resolve discrepancies promptly
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all payable transactions
	Liaise with vendors, internal teams (Procurement, Operations), and Finance to ensure smooth payment processes
	Track outstanding payables and provide regular reports to the Finance Manager
	Ensure compliance with company policies, donor requirements (where applicable), and regulatory standards
	Support tax processes including withholding tax deductions and documentation
	Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities
	Support audits by providing required documentation and schedules


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	2–4 years experience in accounts payable or a similar role, preferably in a fast-paced environment
	Strong understanding of accounting principles and payables processes
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting/ERP systems (experience with Zero is an advantage)
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy
	Good communication and stakeholder management skills
	Ability to prioritize and manage multiple deadlines.


Preferred Qualifications:


	ICAN/ACCA (or in progress)
	Experience in healthcare, logistics, or a high-growth startup
	Familiarity with payment platforms and reconciliation tools.


Key Competencies:


	High level of integrity and accountability
	Strong organizational skills
	Problem-solving and critical thinking
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Collaboration and teamwork.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs / Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

	Sales agents are responsible for driving sales, managing customer relationships, and generating leads, playing a crucial role in a company&#39;s revenue growth.


Prospecting:


	Prospecting is the process of identifying potential customers:

Networking and Building Relationships:


	Building and maintaining relationships is at the heart of sales:

Direct Sales Agent Job at Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited was established on October 8, 1999 to provide efficient and qualitative consulting services to the oil and gas industry as well as banks and financial institutions in the areas of human resources placement and management, human resources consulting services, tax administration and payroll services as well as oil and gas fiscal and contractual terms advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Direct Sales Agent

Job Location:  Nigeria
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Sales agents are responsible for driving sales, managing customer relationships, and generating leads, playing a crucial role in a company's revenue growth.


Prospecting:


	Prospecting is the process of identifying potential customers:
	It involves researching and gathering data to qualify leads and ensuring those leads align with the company’s target market.Proficient prospecting sets the groundwork for future sales success.


Networking and Building Relationships:


	Building and maintaining relationships is at the heart of sales:
	Sales agents must engage in networking opportunities, whether through industry events or online platforms, to foster new relationships and nurture existing ones, creating a broad base of loyal clients.


Conducting Product Demonstrations:


	Sales agents are responsible for effectively showcasing their products:
	Product demonstrations are essential for educating customers on how a product solves their problems.


Negotiating and Closing Sales:


	Once a client is interested, negotiation and closing become paramount:
	Sales agents must navigate price discussions and terms, understanding client needs and ensuring mutual satisfaction.


After-Sales Support:


	After-sales support involves ensuring customer satisfaction post-purchase:
	This includes problem-solving, collecting feedback, and providing additional resources or training to enhance the customer’s experience.


Salary
N93,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
